name: Claudio Roditi
abstract: Claudio Roditi (May 28, 1946 – January 17, 2020) was a Brazilian jazz trumpeter. In 1966 Claudio was named a trumpet finalist at the International Jazz Competition in Vienna, Austria.

about_quotes.speaker: Junior Mance Part II
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewee mentions 'Claudio Roditi' only as part of a list of trumpet players ('Jon Faddis, Claudio Roditi. Roy Hargrove. Every night a different trumpet player.') and does not describe any personal interaction, relationship, or collaboration with him.
about_quotes.doc_title: Junior Mance Part II interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 18, 1999 [interview 2 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Randy Brecker
about_quotes.evidence: So this is where I met a lot of other young musicians my age. People like — unknown then — we were all teenagers: ... Claudio Roditi ... We got to play together a lot
about_quotes.doc_title: Randy Brecker interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, December 12, 2022

about_quotes.speaker: David Finck
about_quotes.evidence: I was recommended to Paquito by Claudio Roditi... And I played in Paquito D’Rivera’s band... both Paquito and Claudio, who are very open and helpful, we’d be playing
about_quotes.doc_title: David Finck interviewed by Monk Rowe, Utica, New York, May 29, 2015

about_quotes.speaker: Wilson "Chembo" Corniel
about_quotes.evidence: I was nominated along with Chucho Valdes, Miguel Zenon, Claudio Roditi, and Geoff Keezer...
about_quotes.doc_title: Wilson "Chembo" Corniel interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, November 15, 2019

about_quotes.speaker: Jim Hall
about_quotes.doc_title: Jim Hall — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
